headlines.title: THAW MAY FILE SUIT TO MAKE N. Y. LET HIM ALONE
headlines.fullText:
Bu Associated Press.
CONCORD, N. H. Sept. 27 .- In order to bring about his legal removal to Pennsylvania in the event the New York authorities request for his extra- dition is denied by Governor Felker, taxpayer's suit against the state of New York la planned by Harry K. Thaw.
Thaw said today a report had reached him from New York that Wil- liam Travers Jerome would take no further steps in the case here if the position refused to grant extradition.
dence In New Hampshire, Thad he was confident a taxpayer's suit te prevent further expense to New York en account of a citizen of another state would bring about his legal removal to his home state.
"And that Is the one thing my moth- er and I wish," he added.

headlines.title: GERLACH CAPTURES CARMEN MEETING FOR FORT WORTH
headlines.fullText: The 1916 convention of the Brother- hood of Railway Carmen of America will be held in Fort Worth. Harry C. Gerlach (Silver Shorty), chief var in- spector for the Frisco, was responsl- ble for bringing the convention D Gerlach, president of Silver Leaf Lanige No. 453, Brotherhood of Rall- way Carmen, was one of the Teams delegates to the convention, now in session at Buffalo, He began a cam- paien several months ago to bring the next convention to Fort Worth, This would return after a brief visit northern citles, carrying the convention in bis "ditty bag."

headlines.title: New York Receives $2,500,000 Tax on J. P. Morgan's Estate
headlines.fullText:
My Associated Press,
ALBANY, N. Y. Sept. 27-State Comptroller Sohmer today received a check for $2,500,009 in payment of the Inheritance tax on a part of the estate of the late J. Pierpont Morgan, The payment indicates a valuation of ap- FaxEm- 50000.000. This, however, does not cover the entire estate. bue with only that part over which an agree- ment has been reached between repre- sentativea of the comptroller and the Morgan executora.
No part of the celebrated Morgan art collection is included in this valuation. as it is still undecided whether this will be turned over to New York City. If the city becomes the owner no tax will be required.

headlines.title: AMERICAN WINS RACE FOR MIKADO MEETING
headlines.subtitle: Francis Burton Harrison Hon ored by Japanese Emperor.
headlines.fullText:
By Associated Press.
TOKIO, Sept. 27-Francis Burton Harrison of New York, newly appoint- won a bard race today to keep his ap- pointment for an audience with the emperor of Japan.
The steamship Manchuria, on which Mr. and Mrs. Harrison mailed from San Francisco, Sept. 1, was delayed by fog. Mr. Harrison landed quickly but just missed the boat train. He caught an express to Tokio and on ar- rival, proceeded by automobile to the palace with the American ambassador. The Japanese court bruke all preces dents in arranging in advance th mourned might rojoin his steamer at Kobe to- Ferom nem Manila, This breaking of precedents **....
Mr. Harrison'a audience with the emperor is regarded here as of great political significance, us a demonstra. tion of Japan's good will and friend- ship toward the United States.

headlines.title: GUNBOAT RETURNS TO FRISCO; THIRTY DESERT
headlines.subtitle: Yorktown Reports Fight Bullets Struck Her Deck in To- polobampo
headlines.fullText:
FRANCISCO, Sept. 27 .- The gan sunboat Yorktown has returned to gan Francisco after a trip to Central Amer- lean and Mexican ports, short thirty members of the marine corps and crew that deserted at various points.

headlines.title: Y. M. C. A. TEAMS GET PRELIMINARY TRY-OUT
headlines.subtitle: Captains Want to Set Standard of Ob- taining One New Member Every Dav.
headlines.fullText:
"Muggsy Metiraw" Smith and "Con. nie Mack" Beckham, the two captains in the Fort Worth Y. M. C. A. cam- paign for new members, are trying out the members of their teams during the next few days to see if they come up to the standard.
The standard is to obtain at least one new member of the association each day of the content. Monday this will announce their respective teams and arrange for the work of the four The end Rules are being printed and will be distributed Monday.
Dallas. Houston and Little Rock are also making extensive preparations for the contest.
